$comment: THB-07 legacy→M3 role mapping table. Reviewed once, applied mechanically by sweep.js, enforced by src/utils/theme/m3Sweep.test.ts. Fallbacks always keep the legacy value — zero visual change until the runtime palette is injected (THB-05).

$cssVarRenamesNotes.--skin-color-primary-contrast-safe: Legacy runtime contrast switch; M3 guarantees on-colour contrast by construction, primary-on-surface readability is guarded by the too-pale warning (UAT-F).
$cssVarRenamesNotes.--skin-color-default: Was the tenant primary in disguise (see settings.scss message-name vars).
$cssVarRenamesNotes.--text-color-contrast-switch: Text/icon on primary background → on-primary role.

$sassVarConversionsNotes.$secondary: rgba(0,0,0,0.9) — semantically body text, NOT the brand secondary → on-surface.

$excludedFilesNotes: settings.scss is the fallback source (definitions stay); mui-variables-mapping.scss is the bridge file, updated by hand in the theme.jsx commit.
$knownFunctionWrappedExceptionsNotes: Sass colour functions cannot wrap a CSS custom property; these pressed-state shades stay compile-time and keep deriving from the legacy values. Documented follow-up if exotic signal seeds need exact pressed shades.
$untouchedVariablesNotes: Context-dependent (text on brand vs plain backgrounds); not part of the documented legacy list and unsafe for a mechanical sweep.
$hexComment: THB-07/#143 hex layer (value-matched to dev token values). Each var(--m3-X, #legacy) resolves to <=JND of the legacy colour on dev TODAY: target token value == legacy (value-match), or token is undefined on dev so the #legacy fallback wins. Hexes with no zero-change unrestricted token are left raw (rawHexNotMigrated). Enforced by m3Sweep.test.ts zero-drift guard.
